what_it_does: It captures a charter for the provider/platform-registry lane, framing an archaeology-grounded plan to extend an existing platform/capability registry (System A), redesign a hand-maintained model catalog (System B) into a file-discovered registry, and integrate the two.
observations: Identifies two orthogonal registry systems today: System A (platform/capability, production-grade, file-discovered Pydantic rows) and System B (model catalog, hand-maintained JSON).
System A already registers Claude Code and gh-cli, and cap:// resolves CapabilityEntry rows.
System B is described as incomplete and not a registry pattern because it lacks file-discovery, self-registration, and create_* helpers.
Proposes a three-move plan: extend System A, redesign System B, and wire A↔B integration.
Lists capture gaps not yet modeled: costs, latency SLOs, safety posture, strengths/weaknesses, observed failures, routing hints, doc links, triggers/activation rules.
Notes cap:// overload between live platform surfaces and aspirational model bindings, suggesting model:// for models.
Defines a lean lane composition with roles and leaves several direction calls open for Tim.
standouts: The core insight that there are two orthogonal registry systems, not one, and the plan addresses both rather than choosing a single approach.
The explicit evidence-not-authority frame and read-only archaeology grounding.
The proposal to introduce a distinct model:// scheme to separate models from platform surfaces.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML front matter (id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, history).
Uses a charter format with sections, bullet lists, and open questions.
Breaks typical brief-item brevity by embedding a detailed multi-system archaeology report and decision framework.
concerns: Several direction calls are left unresolved, so the file is a proposal awaiting confirmation rather than a decided plan.
Some terms (e.g., 'Company/RHM', 'Claude-Design', 'Workspace-Agents') are mentioned without context or definition within the file.
The lane composition mixes role names with process sequencing in a single dense paragraph.
notes: A charter/proposal that uses an archaeology lens to split provider/platform registry work into extending System A, redesigning System B, and integrating them, while surfacing unresolved decisions for Tim.
questions: What is the exact scope and definition of 'Company/RHM', 'Claude-Design', and 'Workspace-Agents' as platform rows?
What criteria will decide whether a platform row belongs in System A versus a provider entry in the redesigned System B?
How will the A↔B integration be implemented technically (e.g., foreign key, reference field, or generated join)?
What is the intended relationship between model:// identifiers and existing cap:// CapabilityEntry rows?
Who or what is 'Tim' in this context, and what authority does the 'LEAD as touchpoint' role carry?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to charter the provider/platform-registry lane; the file does exactly that by documenting archaeology findings, a 3-move plan, gaps, and open decisions.
apparent_themes: Evidence over authority in code archaeology.
Distinguishing live platform surfaces from abstract model/provider capabilities.
Lean, session-based lane composition with adversarial verification.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a shared, archaeology-grounded plan for unifying two existing registry systems, avoid assumptions, and get explicit direction on scope, schema, and naming before building.
novelty: The explicit rejection of both 'build one new registry' and 'merely extend the existing' in favor of a dual-system extend-redesign-integrate plan grounded in a read-only archaeology inventory.
contribution: A structured framing that preserves the two-system reality, enumerates capture gaps, proposes a distinct model:// scheme, and records open decisions so later work can build from evidence rather than assumptions.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard charter item that proposes an archaeology-grounded provider/platform-registry plan: extend the production-grade System A platform/capability registry, redesign the hand-maintained System B model catalog into a file-discovered registry, integrate them, and resolve cap:// overload with a model:// scheme.
intention_for_embedding: To align the team on a dual-system registry consolidation plan and capture unresolved direction calls before implementation.
